Fix per-parameter adaptive learning rate formula and add decay regime markers to plots (#10)

* Initial plan

* Update learning rate to per-parameter adaptive formula and add tau vertical line to plots

- src/training.py: Replace g(t)/N with per-parameter adaptive LR:

  plot_training_curves_with_bands for red dashed vertical line
- experiments/*.py: Pass tau to plotting, update labels to clarify
  constant LR phase before hybrid decay regime
- tests/test_core.py: Add tests for per-parameter LR, eps parameter,
  and adaptive LR formula

fix: revert builder to bun to support bun.lockb

npm ci requires package-lock.json and is incompatible with bun.lockb.
Keep bun for build stage, node:22-slim only for the runner.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: switch builder and runner to node:22-slim to resolve OOM

Bun uses JavaScriptCore (JSC) which does not support NODE_OPTIONS
or V8 heap size flags. Switching to Node.js enables --max-old-space-size
to work correctly for memory control in Kubernetes.

perf: replace O(n) IIFE turn search in Timeline with memoized binary search

The current-turn lookup was an inline IIFE that ran on every render, doing a
linear scan through all turns. During playback currentTime updates at animation
speed (~60fps), so this ran hundreds of times per second on large sessions.

Fix: extract into useMemo keyed on [currentTime, turns]. Since turns are
time-ordered and non-overlapping, use binary search (O(log n)) instead of
linear scan. Falls back to last turn when currentTime is past all turn ranges.

Fix perf regression: limit debug info to relevant subprograms (#8174) (#8177)

PR #7799 added debug info for global variables across all inlined
subprograms, creating O(subprograms x globals) debug instructions. This
caused 2-3x slower compilation for shaders with many globals and
deeply-inlined functions.

Fix: Instead of collecting ALL subprograms for a function (walking every
instruction), only collect subprograms from the alloca's actual users --
the instructions that reference the specific global variable. This
preserves the debug info feature (globals visible in inlined scopes that
use them) while eliminating work for unrelated scopes.

The 'Lower static global into Alloca' pass is 3.8x faster on a stress
test (41ms -> 11ms), and overall debug compilation is ~10% faster.

Fixes #8174
